title: mentha spicata subsp. glabrata
intro: Mentha spicata subsp. glabrata, also known as glabrous peppermint, is a subspecies of peppermint that is characterized by its smooth, hairless leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and digestive properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of menthol and menthone. The health benefits of Mentha spicata subsp. glabrata include relief from nausea, headaches, and indigestion, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, and is often combined with other herbs like ginger and lemon balm to enhance its medicinal properties.
